mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding labor income and aggregate demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day S&P 500 returns follow rising PAYEMS in fewer than 55% of months, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 2.0% maintains restrictive monetary conditions that elevate corporate borrowing costs and compress equity risk premiums, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%, S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) confirm broad-based contraction across consumption and manufacturing, amplifying recession risk and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and INDPRO do not precede negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of coincident episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is already below 4.5%, further declines signal an overheating labor market that intensifies wage inflation and Federal Reserve tightening expectations, raising discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: During periods when UNRATE is below 4.5% and falling, S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) signals rising manufacturing profitability and capacity utilization, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rising INDPRO fails to coincide with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of months.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1200 thousand units indicate a contraction in residential construction and deteriorating household confidence, which reduces cyclical demand and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when HOUST is below 1200.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) combined with a stagnant or elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) produces restrictive real monetary policy that squeezes corporate margins and equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising CPIAUCSL and elevated FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative 20-day S&P 500 returns with a Rank IC above 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recoveries, upside surprises in real GDP (GDPC1) signal accelerating aggregate demand that outpaces prior expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In quarters classified as post-recession recovery, higher GDPC1 growth does not predict positive 20-day S&P 500 returns with a t-statistic greater than 2.
